message:
refactor: replace unstable Duration::from_mins/from_hours with from_secs

Duration::from_mins() and Duration::from_hours() are not yet stabilized
in Rust's standard library (still behind #![feature(duration_constructors)]).
This codebase targets stable Rust, so all ~80 call sites across 40 files
are replaced with the stable equivalent:

  - Duration::from_mins(N)  → Duration::from_secs(N * 60)
  - Duration::from_hours(N) → Duration::from_secs(N * 3600)

Affected subsystems (production defaults + test fixtures):
  - Combinators: bulkhead queue timeout, circuit breaker reset/half-open,
    rate limiter period
  - Messaging: Kafka transaction timeout, JetStream stream/consumer config
  - Networking: DNS cache TTL, QUIC idle timeout, WebSocket client
  - Runtime: deadline monitor intervals
  - Observability: obligation tracker window, task inspector
  - Server: graceful shutdown grace period, CORS max_age
  - Supervision: restart backoff, one-for-one/rest-for-one strategies
  - Time: timer wheel spoke config, interval scheduling
  - Distributed: recovery retry, snapshot config
  - Other: actor, app, channel, config, conformance, evidence,
    fs metadata, gen_server, gRPC client, HTTP pool/listener/server

Purely mechanical — no behavioral changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -254,7 +254,7 @@ impl Default for DistributedRegionConfig {
             min_quorum: 2,
             replication_factor: 3,
             init_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),
-            recovery_timeout: Duration::from_mins(1),
+            recovery_timeout: Duration::from_secs(1 * 60),
             allow_degraded: true,
             read_consistency: ConsistencyLevel::One,
             write_consistency: ConsistencyLevel::Quorum,
